_meta.title: Wire CoCoR Grammar callbacks to V4 consumers
_meta.rationale: Closes gaps surfaced by /understand-explain on TDM.atg callbacks. Five fixes: (1) re-route tlsbSocP.h's CoCoR include from the dev/test copy under SocP/TDM_Compiler/ to the production copy under SysTools/CoCoR/ (matches the literal `#include "CoCoR\cocInterpreter.h"`); (2) record that tlsSocP.h inherits/depends on tlsbSocP.h; (3) record that TDM.atg generates Parser.cpp/.h/Scanner.cpp/.h via Coco.exe; (4) link the SysTools/CoCoR Parser.cpp to its cocInterpreter.h sibling so the production runtime is no longer orphaned; (5) add an inheritance edge tlsbSocP.h -> SysTools/CoCoR/cocInterpreter.h.

edges_to_remove.reason: Misrouted: include path `CoCoR\cocInterpreter.h` resolves to SysTools/CoCoR/, not SocP/TDM_Compiler/ (the latter is a standalone test app).

edges_to_add.note: class tlsbSocP : public cocInterpreter, public tlsbVirtualFunc
class tlsSocP : public tlsInterpreter, public tlsbSocP — overrides every On* callback
Coco/R input grammar — Parser.cpp/.h are regenerated from this file via Coco.exe
Coco/R input grammar — generates Scanner.cpp from CHARACTERS/TOKENS/IGNORE sections
Same/equivalent TDMSkriptPlus grammar drives the production Parser.cpp in SysTools/CoCoR/
Parser's semantic-action callbacks emit through gen-> (a cocInterpreter* set by cocInterpreter::Parse before parser.Parse())
Parser::Parse() invokes the top-level grammar production TDMSkriptPlus()
cocInterpreter::Parse() wires gen=this, tab=new RuntimeSymbolTable(), then parser.Parse()
